author: Augustine of Hippo

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XIV. 18–21.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After the promise of the Holy Spirit, lest any should suppose that the Lord was to give Him, as it were, in place of Himself, in any such way as that He Himself would not likewise be with them, He added the words: “I will not leave you orphans; I will come to you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Orphani [Greek] are pupilli [parent-less children] in Latin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The one is the Greek, the other the Latin name of the same thing: for in the psalm where we read, “Thou art the helper of the fatherless” [in the Latin version, pupillo], the Greek has orphano.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, although it was not the Son of God that adopted sons to His Father, or willed that we should have by grace that same Father, who is His Father by nature, yet in a sense it is paternal feelings toward us that He Himself displays, when He declares, “I will not leave you orphans; I will come to you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the same way He calls us also the children of the bridegroom, when He says, “The time will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall the children of the bridegroom fast.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who is the bridegroom, but Christ the Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He then goes on to say, “Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How so? the world saw Him then; for under the name of the world are to be understood those of whom He spake above, when saying of the Holy Spirit, “Whom the world cannot receive, because it seeth Him not, neither knoweth Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was plainly visible to the carnal eyes of the world, while manifest in the flesh; but it saw not the Word that lay hid in the flesh: it saw the man, but it saw not God: it saw the covering, but not the Being within.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as, after the resurrection, even His very flesh, which He exhibited both to the sight and to the handling of His own, He refused to exhibit to others, we may in this way perhaps understand the meaning of the words, “Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more; but ye shall see me: because I live, ye shall live also.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is meant by the words, “Because I live, ye shall live also”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why did He speak in the present tense of His own living, and in the future of theirs, but just by way of promise that the life also of the resurrection-body, as it preceded in His own case, would certainly follow in theirs?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And as His own resurrection was in the immediate future, He put the word in the present tense to signify its speedy approach: but of theirs, as delayed till the end of the world, He said not, ye live; but, “ye shall live.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With elegance and brevity, therefore, by means of two words, one of them in the present tense and the other in the future, He gave the promise of two resurrections, to wit, His own in the immediate future, and ours as yet to come in the end of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because I live,” He says, “ye shall live also:” because He liveth, therefore shall we live also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as by man is death, by man also is the resurrection of the dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as in Adam all die, even so in Christ shall all be made alive.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As it is only through the former that every one is liable to death, it is only through Christ that any one can attain unto life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because we did not live, we are dead; because He lived, we shall live also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We were dead to Him, when we lived to ourselves; but, because He died in our behalf, He liveth both for Himself and for us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, because He liveth, we shall live also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For while we were able of ourselves to attain unto death, it is not of ourselves also that life can come into our possession.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In that day,” He says, “ye shall know that I am in my Father, and ye in me, and I in you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what day, but in that whereof He said, “Ye shall live also”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For then will it be that we can see what we believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For even now is He in us, and we in Him: this we believe now, but then shall we also know it; although what we know even now by faith, we shall know then by actual vision.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as long as we are in the body, as it now is, to wit, corruptible, and encumbering to the soul, we live at a distance from the Lord; for we walk by faith, not by sight.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then accordingly it will be by sight, for we shall see Him as He is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if Christ were not even now in us, the apostle would not say, “And if Christ be in you, the body is dead indeed because of sin; but the spirit is life because of righteousness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that we are also in Him even then, He makes sufficiently clear, when He says, “I am the vine, ye are the branches.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly in that day, when we shall be living the life, whereby death shall be swallowed up, we shall know that He is in the Father, and we in Him, and He in us; for then shall be completed that very state which is already in the present begun by Him, that He should be in us, and we in Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that hath my commmandments,” He adds, “and keepeth them, he it is that loveth me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that hath [them] in his memory, and keepeth them in his life; who hath them orally, and keepeth them morally; who hath them in the ear, and keepeth them in deed; or who hath them in deed, and keepeth them by perseverance;—“he it is,” He says, “that loveth me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By works is love made manifest as no fruitless application of a name.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And he that loveth me,” He says, “shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what is this, “I will love”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it as if He were then only to love, and loveth not at present?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how could the Father love us apart from the Son, or the Son apart from the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Working as They do inseparably, how can They love apart?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He said, “I will love him,” in reference to that which follows, “and I will manifest myself to him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I will love, and will manifest;” that is, I will love to the very extent of manifesting.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this has been the present aim of His love, that we may believe, and keep hold of the commandment of faith; but then His love will have this for its object, that we may see, and get that very sight as the reward of our faith: for we also love now, by believing in that which we shall see hereafter; but then shall we love in the sight of that which now we believe.
